entries.description: Registered session 1 via start_session (engine=claude, provider=anthropic); read spec.md, the module-lifecycle-simplification verdict, Set 098/100 change-logs, and gitScaffold.ts/moduleAuthoring.ts to plan the reuse.

Extended buildProjectStructureNoPrompt: a Build call that just created docs/modules.yaml now also scaffolds the real 'default' module + its two lifecycle sets via scaffoldNewModule + scaffoldModuleLifecycleSets (Set 087/098 writers, reused verbatim, never modified). New scaffoldDefaultModuleAndLifecycleSets() + BuildStructureSeams.scaffoldDefaultModule test seam in gitScaffold.ts.

Gated the new scaffold strictly on result.written.includes(MODULES_MANIFEST_DISPLAY) (the manifest having been CREATED this exact call) rather than mere emptiness, so a Build re-run (manifest already present, reported in `skipped`) makes no module/set writes.
The same gate means a legacy repo whose docs/modules.yaml already exists (however empty) is left exactly as found by a later Build call -- no forced migration, matching the verdict's decision 4.

Added the Default-is-Class1 short paragraph to both docs/templates/consumer-bootstrap/getting-started.md.template and start-here.md.template (human-facing and orchestrator-facing cold-start docs); regenerated the cold-start golden fixtures (UPDATE_GOLDEN=1) and the Python cold-start acceptance test both pass.

New tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/gitScaffoldDefaultModule.test.ts: real-fs tests for scaffoldDefaultModuleAndLifecycleSets (fresh-scaffold end-state, no-manifest edge case, refused re-call safety) plus fake-seam wiring tests for buildProjectStructureNoPrompt's gating (fresh vs pre-existing manifest, both Full and Lightweight tiers, plus one real-writer end-to-end wiring test). 8 new tests, all passing.

Live dogfood (s1-dogfood.md): drove the REAL compiled buildProjectStructureNoPrompt against a cold-start empty scratch folder (only the network pip install faked), then the real moduleAuthoring.js writers through the full Class1 loop -- Build (default + Sets 001/002) -> re-run Build (idempotent, no new writes) -> rename (default->greeter, zero orphans) -> delete (both lifecycle sets removed, manifest entry gone) -> re-add (payments, fresh pair). All 6 named checks PASS.

Full suite: extension unit 1613/1613 (up from 1605 baseline, +8 new), tsc --noEmit clean, eslint unchanged pre-existing baseline (7 errors in untouched test files, matching Set 100's recorded baseline), esbuild clean, Python cold-start acceptance test (both tiers) passing.
Full ai_router pytest suite: 3030 passed, 6 skipped in 498.17s -- unchanged baseline (no Python files touched this session), matching Set 100's recorded baseline.

Phased verification discovery round 1 (fan-out K=2, gpt-5-6): ISSUES_FOUND, 4 blocking Major findings (both discovery calls independently found the same legacy-repo-mutation bug in different framings; plus VSIX-dogfood-completeness and Work-Explorer-tree-test-completeness findings). Supplementary discovery pass: VERIFIED, 0 new findings.

Remediated all 4 merged findings (s1-remediation-round-1.md): (1&2) scaffoldDefaultModuleAndLifecycleSets now refuses whenever docs/session-sets/ already has any set dirs, regardless of manifest-creation signal -- fixes a real bug where a legacy repo upgrading and re-running Build would get an unsolicited Default module; (3) packaged the real VSIX (vsce package, 43 files, clean) and added a genuine Playwright Layer-3 spec driving a real VS Code Electron instance against the scaffold's exact fixture shape; (4) added Work-Explorer tree-model tests (computeVisibleModules/buildVisibleModulePayloads) for both tiers plus the legacy-pseudo-module non-interference case. Suite after remediation: extension unit 1617/1617 (+4), Playwright Layer 3 27/27 (+1, 0 regressions), tsc/eslint/esbuild clean.

close_session backstop ran its own verification (round 4) and re-raised the VSIX-walkthrough finding with a more literal demand (install the .vsix, drive native dialogs), disputing round 3 acceptance. Per operator direction, routed a neutral third-party opinion (gemini-pro, excluding anthropic+openai) rather than grinding another round or overriding unilaterally -- raw at s1-third-opinion-vsix-dispute.json.

Third opinion: the verifier is literal demand (install VSIX + native dialog automation) is technically infeasible given this repo is own documented constraints, but identified a real, narrower, previously-untested gap -- no test proved the registered dabbler.setupNewProject command callback actually dispatches into buildProjectStructureNoPrompt. Fixed with a new offline wiring test in gitScaffoldCore.test.ts (captures the real registered callback, invokes it with an explicit tier arg against a missing-python fixture, proves the friendly pre-flight error only fires if the full command-to-scaffold chain executed). Suite: 1618/1618 (+1). s1-remediation-round-2.md.

Remediation-review round 5 (gpt-5-6): 3 of 4 ledger findings fix-accepted (legacy-repo gate, VSIX/render coverage, Work Explorer end-state tests); L5 (the literal VSIX walkthrough) fix-REJECTED -- the offline stub-callback wiring test proves dispatch, not the real interactive journey. Operator directed continued work on the real walkthrough (model upgraded to Fable) instead of adjudicating it away.

Built the REAL first-run walkthrough the spec names verbatim: vsix-first-run-walkthrough.spec.ts drives a real VS Code Electron instance through the actual UI end to end -- real Build button in the Getting Started webview (real venv + network pip install, the new default-module scaffold), real Command Palette + QuickPick + InputBoxes + modal confirms for rename -> delete -> re-add, tree asserted after every step. PASSING (23s warm). Five real automation blockers discovered and documented (activation-on-view-reveal, client-side budget validation silently no-ops Build, F1 command-prefix wiped by fill(), placeholder-vs-text QuickPick waits, window.dialogStyle defaults NATIVE -- fixed via --enable-smoke-test-driver, with an additive extraArgs param on launchVSCode). The earlier infeasibility judgment (mine + the routed third opinion) was WRONG; the verifier was right to hold the line. s1-remediation-round-3.md.
